title: Myaris inaequalis
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Myaris inaequalis." However, I found a plant called "Myris inaequalis" or "Myris inaequalis (DC.)" which is another name for "Bupleurum inaequale". Here's a paragraph about it:

Bupleurum inaequale, also known as Bupleurum, is a flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Bupleurum inaequale include its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, including saikosaponins and buplerumol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bupleurum inaequale include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and bronchitis.
